summary: Matched three previously stubbed functions in fighter and player trick code: ft_800898C0, ft_8008A324, and pl_80037ECC. The PR also corrected related prototypes and refined pl_800386D8_t layout so the newly matched player function can access the attack-count field at offset 0x1A8 directly.
what_changed: Implemented ft_800898C0 as a boolean predicate over Fighter x2070.x2073 values 113, 114, and 99, or Fox side-appeal usage via ftFx_AppealS_CheckIfUsed.
Implemented ft_8008A324 as a small wrapper that calls ft_8008A348(gobj, -1.0f).
Implemented pl_80037ECC to get the Fighter from an HSD_GObj, look up the player's total attack-count structure with Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr(ft->player_id), and increment field x1A8.
Changed ft_800898C0 declaration from int to bool in ft_0892.h.
Changed pl_80037ECC declaration from taking void to taking HSD_GObj* in pltrick.h.
Split pl_800386D8_t padding at offset 0x1A8 and added an explicit int x1A8 field before x1AC.
Adjusted pltrick.c includes from an angle-bracket local include to quoted local header style and added needed ft/inlines.h and pl/player.h includes.
smart_moves: Used an existing shared helper, ft_8008A348, to match ft_8008A324 instead of duplicating transition logic.
Converted ft_800898C0 to bool because the implementation is a true predicate and returns only true or false.
Exposed the pl_800386D8_t offset 0x1A8 member explicitly, avoiding opaque byte-array indexing in pl_80037ECC.
Added only the headers required by the new implementations: ftFox appeal-side check for fighter logic, ft/inlines.h for GET_FIGHTER, and pl/player.h for Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr.
decomp_lessons: Small wrapper functions may match cleanly by forwarding to a known transition/state helper with a literal argument, as ft_8008A324 does with -1.0f.
When a function increments or accesses a field inside previously anonymous padding, split the struct padding at the exact offset and name the field rather than using pointer arithmetic.
Predicate-like unknown functions should have their header prototypes corrected to bool when the matched body returns boolean values.
Function signatures in headers should be updated as soon as the matched body reveals a real parameter, as with pl_80037ECC taking HSD_GObj*.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based names for still-unknown functions and fields: ft_800898C0, ft_8008A324, pl_80037ECC, pl_800386D8_t, and x1A8.
Used established project type names Fighter_GObj, HSD_GObj, Fighter, and pl_800386D8_t.
Kept struct fields named by offset, such as x2070.x2073 and x1A8, where semantic names are not yet evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched a boolean chain with explicit equality checks against x2070.x2073 values 113, 114, and 99, followed by an OR with ftFx_AppealS_CheckIfUsed.
Matched a trivial thunk/wrapper by calling ft_8008A348 with constant -1.0f.

Matched player-stat update logic by routing from HSD_GObj to Fighter via GET_FIGHTER, then to a per-player stats pointer via Player_GetTotalAttackCountPtr(ft->player_id), then incrementing offset 0x1A8.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
No PR body or human rationale was included in the provided excerpt, so intent beyond matching these functions is inferred only from the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find callers of ft_800898C0 to determine semantic meaning of x2070.x2073 values 113, 114, and 99.
Find all accesses to pl_800386D8_t x1A8 to determine whether it is specifically total attack count or a narrower statistic.
Investigate whether ft_8008A324 has a higher-level semantic name tied to neutral/wait transition behavior.
